description_long: This house features a prominent gable roof with a deep brown color, extending well beyond the walls to provide ample overhang. The front facade is defined by two sturdy columns supporting a covered porch and balcony, with a blue-gray railing and a recessed entryway beneath. The upper level includes a balcony area with a single window, while the lower level has two windows flanking the main entrance. The rear of the house is plain, with no visible windows or doors, while the sides each have two symmetrically placed windows. The exterior walls are finished in a light beige color, and the structure sits on a simple concrete base. The overall design is functional and straightforward, with minimal decorative elements.
description: A two-story beige house with a large brown gable roof, front porch supported by columns, and a balcony above the main entrance. Windows are symmetrically arranged on the sides, and the rear is windowless.
description_view_0: Front view showing the covered porch, balcony with blue-gray railing, two supporting columns, and main entrance beneath the overhang.
description_view_1: Rear view displaying a plain beige wall with two windows and no entryway or decorative features.
description_view_2: Side view highlighting the full expanse of the gable roof and a solid beige wall with no windows or doors.
description_view_3: Side view with two symmetrically placed windows and a partial view of the balcony and porch structure.
attribute_entryway_design: Recessed main entry beneath a covered porch, supported by two columns, with a balcony and overhanging roof above.
